observations.recommendation: willful enforcement of existing ordinances and their fines and penalties to effectively and efficiently implement SWM, especially on the segregation, collecting, and disposal of solid wastes;
observations.managementAction: The LGU of Buhi, through MENRO, shall establish proper institutional coordination among all government and non-government stakeholders to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the SWM plan, laws, and other local ordinances in the municipality of Buhi.
observations.reason: There is a lack of institutional coordination among the Sangguniang Bayan of Buhi, MENRO, PSO, PNP, and Barangay Councils in enforcing existing laws and ordinances, as well as in the effective and efficient implementation of solid waste management programs, projects, and activities.

observations.recommendation: employment of appropriate action to address the delayed expansion of the garbage collection coverage of the LGU to catch up with the target number of barangays per year in accordance with the SWM Plan.
observations.managementAction: Insufficient personnel to manage operations, lack of garbage trucks for collection, and neglect by some barangay officials in fulfilling their responsibilities and assisting MENRO with solid waste collection at the barangay level. The LGU of Buhi, through MENRO and other stakeholders, shall ensure the strict and full implementation of the Ecological Solid Waste Management System in the municipality of Buhi.
observations.reason: Lack of IEC and institutional coordination with stakeholders.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a) Through the GSO and OAIAS, immediately reconcile the discrepancies
between the balances of the PPE sub-accounts in the GL and those in the
RPCPPE, and thereafter submit the reconciled report to the COA Audit
Team;
b) Through the Municipal Engineering Office, provide assistance to the GSO,
OAIAS and all other concerned departments/offices in locating and
identifying missing/unidentified PPE items, if any, particularly those
related to infrastructure projects, and thereafter, submit a detailed
accomplishment report on the findings;
c) Determine and finalize the legal and operational status of the
aforementioned two (2) heavy equipment, and thereafter, effect all
necessary accounting adjustments entries to accurately reflect their
condition and ownership in the books of accounts;
d) Require all departments/offices to comply with the minimum
documentation and reporting requirements prescribed under Section 57,
Volume 2, of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS) for Local Government Units, including the submission of a
summary of accounts and corresponding values for each item reported, to
ensure clarity and completeness;
e) Institutionalize the regular reconciliation of PPE accounts to ensure all
acquisitions and disposals of PPE are properly accounted for in both the
financial statements and other relevant property records; and
f) Enforce strict adherence to Section 114, Volume I of the MNGAS and other
applicable government regulations on the management, utilization, and
reporting of public property, in order to avoid the recurrence of
discrepancies in PPE records.

We recommended that Management:

a) Through the OAIAS, reconcile the discrepancies between the balances of
the PPE accounts recorded in the GL and those reflected in the PPELC,
and thereafter submit the reconciled report to the COA Audit Team, for
evaluation and verification;
b) Conduct regular reconciliation of PPE accounts to ensure that all
acquisitions, transfers and dispositions of assets are properly documented,
recorded and accurately reflected in the FS and other pertinent accounting
records; and
c) strictly observe and implement the provisions of Section 114 and 120 of the
MNGAS, as well as other relevant government accounting and auditing
regulations, to prevent future discrepancies and strengthen the integrity of
PPE account management and reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:

a) Establish efficient planning and coordination with the Municipal
Treasurer to avoid delays in the submission of pertinent documents and/or
reports, to ensure proper recording in the books of accounts and accurate
presentation of the financial statements;
b) Expedite the submission of the corresponding DVs and supporting
documents for the identified unrecorded disbursements, in coordination
with the Municipal Treasurer, to COA, for proper audit and verification;
c) Disclose the unreconciled items under the Cash in Bank – Local Currency
account, amounting to ₱2,629,977.45, in the Notes to the Financial
Statements to ensure the integrity of financial reporting and to reflect the
Municipality’s true financial position; and
d) Facilitate the preparation of adjusting journal entries for valid reconciling
items, such as those identified and discussed, in compliance with Section
3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to
maintain and reconcile the prescribed records such as SLCs and SCs to
ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and
issuance of supplies;
b) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the periodic physical count
of supplies or property and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI
to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for
the first and second semesters, respectively;
c) Require the General Services Officer to submit consolidated SSMI and
RIS to the Accounting Office at least on a monthly basis; and
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the necessary supporting
documents to be attached to the SSMI and RIS as proof of its distribution
to qualified beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a) Comply with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 141 of the Volume
III of the NGAS Manual for LGUs which state that claims against
government funds should be supported with complete documentation and
that the recording of liabilities should arise only from goods delivered or
services rendered, and make the necessary adjustments for those payable
not covered with legal and valid claims; and
b) Remit the DST and CGT to the BIR and assess the validity of the claims
which are outstanding for more than two (2) years, otherwise revert said
balances to the related account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer
to:

a) Require all collecting officers to remit their collections intact and daily or
within the period prescribed by the existing regulations; and
b) Avoid issuing additional stubs of accountable forms to collectors unless the
previous stubs issued were accounted for and remitted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

a) Timely submit the CRAAF duly accompanied by the individual monthly
RAAFs of the AOs in compliance with Section 86.g, Book II of the LTOM
on the control of official receipts and other accountable forms which
reiterates Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual; and
b) Enforce strict compliance of the accountable officers on the monthly
submission of duly accomplished RAAF in accordance with Section 86.g,
Book II of the LTOM and Section 50 of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Management:

a) Ensure the proper granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash
advances in accordance with the applicable provisions of COA Circular
No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, particularly enforcing no additional
cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the
previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting
thereof is made, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances;
b) Consider designating additional disbursing officer/s who shall handle
payments of the numerous payrolls and other expenses for various
activities of the LGU so that the duties and responsibilities on cash
advances are not only lodged to only one accountable officer;
c) Allot sufficient fidelity bond to cover the amount of cash advances granted
to designated disbursing officers in case of loss or misuse;
d) Request for the write-off of dormant accounts, aging 10 years onwards in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and 
e) Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to COA the list of accountable
officers, officers, and employees with the amount of their past due accounts
or unliquidated cash advances along with their complete names and
complete present or verified address, for issuance of demand letters by
COA.

We recommended that Management, through the GSO and all other
concerned offices of the Local Government Unit (LGU), ensure strict adherence to
Section 124, Volume 1, of the MNGAS, as well as all other pertinent provisions of the
said manual governing property and asset management, to ensure the timely
preparation and submission of the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a) Establish efficient planning and coordination with the Municipal
Treasurer to avoid delays in submitting pertinent documents and to
ascertain proper recording in the books of accounts and the accurate
presentation of the financial statements; and
b) Facilitate the prompt reconciliation of the identified inter-agency payable
accounts to present the accurate balances, and closely oversee the timely
and complete recognition of subsequent monthly withholdings and
corresponding remittances in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes on
unpaid obligations as of year-end are remitted in the period when the
related asset or expense accounts are recorded or accrued to ensure strict
compliance RR No. 2-98 and RMC No. 47-2023 to avoid payment of the
corresponding penalties;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to review and correct the withholding
tax rates applied on the transactions of the Municipality and remit
deficiency and correct withholding taxes to ensure compliance with the
provisions of the Tax Code and RR No. 2-98;
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to check
and monitor suppliers of the Municipality whose transactions will
potentially exceed the VAT threshold of ₱3,000,000.00 as well as initiate
appropriate action to conform and update their status in conformity with
the requirements of the Tax Code; and
d) Initiate appropriate action to ensure that all transactions with suppliers
are levied with applicable and correct taxes, for remittance to BIR in
accordance with the Tax Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers
containing details of transactions of the accounts and projects funded by
trust fund such as the LGSF-SBDP from NTF-ELCAC;
b) Direct the concerned Local Finance Committee on the compliance of the
preparation of quarterly utilization reports of LGSF-SBDP from NTF-
ELCAC fund transfers, in accordance to the prescribed format (Annex C)
in the DBM LBC, for submission to concerned offices and posting to
conspicuous places and LGU’s website;
c) Initiate appropriate action on the proper maintenance of the LGU’s
website, for updated posting online of LGU’s implemented PPAs, notices
and other information in line with government’s promotion of
transparency and accountability.

We reiterated our recommendation in the prior years that Management
through the office of the Municipal Engineer and MPDC adopt strategies to maximize
the utilization of the LDF by implementing the development projects within the
targeted timeframe and develop a concrete catch-up plan for the delayed projects of
LDF.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
accounting adjusting entries to recognize the completed LDF projects in the books of
the LGU.

We recommended that the Management:

a) Ensure that properly accomplished monthly utilization reports and
RAAOs are submitted and maintained in accordance to Item Nos. 5.1.4
and 5.1.5 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for
proper regular monitoring and audit of the fund;
b) Require the concerned local officials to strictly comply with the budgeting
and utilization policies, rules and regulations governing the use of prior
years unexpended balance of LDRRMF by securing approved revised
LDRRMFIP supported with SB Resolution authorizing the
reprogramming of the LDRRMF unexpended balance from prior years;
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate immediately the preparation
of necessary accounting entries to recognize/capitalize the acquired DRRM
equipment totaling ₱7,248,380.00 in the GF from the TF accounts.
d) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to promptly submit the necessary
supporting documents to substantiate payments made on the identified
transactions with lacking and deficient supporting documents charged to
the LDRRMF to the Audit Team, for verification;
e) Ensure that granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance is strictly
in accordance with the prescribe rules and regulations and complete with
documentary requirements, to avoid committing irregular transactions,
which may be subjected to issuance of appropriate audit notice, if
warranted; and
f) Direct concerned management officials to ensure that only DRRM related
expenses are charged to the LDRRMF to maximize the purpose and
achievement of the goals and objectives of the fund and to avoid unwanted
audit sanctions that may be issued due to utilization of the fund for non-
related DRRM expenses.

We recommended that Management assign an RC or SL code to the GFPS,
whichever is deemed more practical, to ensure proper identification of GAD-related
expenses and to facilitate the monitoring, accounting, and preparation of GAD-
related reports, which are essential for assessing the effectiveness and efficiency of the
Municipality’s gender mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended that the Management:

a) Ensure that all schedules of activities of the Municipality are properly
monitored to give lead time to the procurement process so that it is timely
undertaken, to avoid resorting to reimbursements; and
b) Ensure that all disbursements are properly supported with adequate
evidence of its validity in accordance with the required documentation set
forth in the applicable provisions of government procurement law and
other related rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management:

a) Formulate uniform policy and procedures in selecting and hiring JO
workers taking into consideration the existing law, rules and regulations;
b) Require the HRMO to prepare a written policy adopting a schedule of
salary rates of workers based on qualifications as well as the nature of
work/assignment to be performed;
c) Use appropriate contract forms that outline the specific duties and
responsibilities of the hired JO workers;
d) Require the JO/COS workers to provide accomplishment reports which
are quantifiable and traceable to their department’s/section’s
performance targets;
e) Strictly comply with the documentary requirements in COA Circular No.
2012-001; and
f) Withhold the appropriate taxes on the payments to JO/COS workers and
require them to comply with the BIR registration requirements set forth
in RMC 51-2018.

We recommended that Management to direct the Municipal Engineer and
MPDC or any other responsible Local Department Heads to consistently prepare and
submit duly Approved List of all on-going government projects/programs/activities
(PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year to the Supervising
Auditor and Audit Team Leader at the beginning of the year pursuant to Item No.
2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 for verification, updated if
need arises;

We also recommended strict compliance to the provisions of Section 3 of the
same COA Circular on posting requirements thru a standard Project Signboard as
designed and its notification to COA, as well as the submission of the duly signed
Monthly Monitoring Report and/or a Statement of Work Accomplishments (SWA)
based on the interval of verification (%) percentage provided for in the Table.

We recommended that Management:

a) Initiate coordination with the concerned contractors regarding the
deficiencies noted, and formally require them to undertake the necessary
remedial measures to prevent further deterioration of the affected
projects, imposing appropriate sanctions, if warranted, including but not
limited to blacklisting of contractors for failure to comply with their
warranty obligations or to effect necessary repairs;
b) Implement a more rigorous validation and review process of construction
plans and designs prior to project implementation, in order to minimize
the need for variation orders and ensure alignment with actual project
requirements;
c) Furnish the Audit Team with the official communication or documentation
reflecting coordination with the Department of Public Works and
Highways (DPWH) or any other pertinent agency regarding the repair
plans for Project No. 11, for verification; and
d) Submit to the Audit Team a comprehensive written report or status update
detailing the corrective actions taken by Management in response to the
deficiencies observed in the subject infrastructure projects, for re-
validation.

We recommended that the Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to expedite the submission of the corresponding DVs and supporting documents of the identified unrecorded disbursements to COA, for proper audit and verification; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the preparation of the adjusting journal entries for valid reconciling items such as the unrecorded disbursements in compliance with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

assign one accounting personnel who will reconcile the difference of ₱15.80 million of the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds for all funds; and
b)  effect necessary adjustments on said accounts to reflect their correct and reliable balances as of December 31, 2020 and onwards.

We recommended that Management:

facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements to secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) under the name of the Municipality of Buhi for the 15 parcels of land to support claims of ownership and to avoid loss of properties in case other interested parties may claim the same in the future; and
b)  include in the ensuing years’ appropriations the fund required to expedite the application for the land titles under the name of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management enhance its compliance with local roads asset management system by requiring the:

General Services Officer to submit the required reports under COA Circular No.  2015-008 using the prescribed format;
Municipal Accountant to make the proper disclosure of Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Inventory of Local Road Network and the accounting records.

We recommended that Management enhance its compliance with the policies and procedures by directing the:

GSO and the Accounting Office to continue to reconcile the property and book records and immediately effect adjustments, if any; and
Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory taking of all properties of the Municipality and prepare the corresponding reports using the prescribed format and to be submitted within the period provided in the NGAS.

We recommended that Management secure an insurance for all insurable properties of the Municipality from the GIF of the GSIS to protect the properties against loss or damage, where in case of loss or total destruction, the Municipality can recover the costs of the asset/s.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

present to COA for verification the corresponding unpaid DV, approved ObR, (PO) or Contract, and/or creditor’s billing statement or invoice of the subject Financial Liabilities accounts totaling to ₱5.29 million in compliance with the pertinent provisions of PD 1445; and
revert the recorded liabilities and restore the cash account of the Payable accounts not covered with valid claims.

We recommended that Management demand for the termination of agreements entered into with delinquent fish cage operators with terms exceeding three years and implement dismantling of the corresponding fish cages or fish pens, notwithstanding the filing of appropriate case in order to collect the arrearages.

We also recommended that Management require the:
Municipal Lake Development Office to:
update the database of fish cages owners/operators and its corresponding occupied area and reconcile the same with the records maintained in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer; and
conduct regular inspection of the municipal fish cages and other facilities to ensure they are properly maintained and compliant with the agreement and ordinance.
Municipal Treasury Office to:
update the assessment of uncollected fees and implement strategies to collect the same; and
conduct periodic review of its collection efficiency to be assured of improved performance and higher revenues generated.
Sangguniang Bayan to revisit the existing Municipal Revenue Code in order to address the deficiencies noted and come up with a comprehensive ordinance that will maximize the revenue of the Municipality from fishery operations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Office in coordination with the Offices in charge of the supplies, goods and materials utilization/distribution, to facilitate the submission of the SSMI together with the Distribution List of the goods consumed/distributed in CY 2020 to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team in order to support the recorded Expense Accounts totaling ₱37.43 million at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to consistently follow the procedures prescribed by RA 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 09-2009 in particular, in the procurement of goods through shopping and small value procurement.
We recommended that Management direct its officers to fully implement the GAD activities and the implementation should be monitored regularly by the GFPS.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Optimize the utilization of the 20% DF and ensure that programs, projects and activities are completed on time; and
Monitor and guarantee the completion of continuing projects started in prior years and resolve the causes of the delayed implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated previous year’s recommendation that the Management require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds for all funds by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers; and
effect necessary adjustments on said accounts to reflect their correct and reliable balances as of December 31, 2019 and onwards.

We recommended that Management direct the:
GSO and the Accounting Office to reconcile their respective records, investigate the differences noted in the two reports and immediately effect adjustments, if any; and
creation of an inventory committee who shall conduct the complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the Municipality on a full time basis within a specified period and preparesubmit the corresponding reports thereon to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year as mandated in Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I.

We recommended that Management require the:
General Services Officer to:
1. prepare a Report on Local Road Network;
2. maintain local road inventory and road map; and
3. maintain property cards for all roads and its components.
Acting Municipal Accountant to:
1.   maintain subsidiary ledger cards for roads and road components for every road network;
2. prepare lapsing schedule showing the computation of the depreciation of each component of the Road Network System account;
3.   disclose the total Road Network System account in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
4.  make the necessary adjusting entries on repair and maintenance recorded to Road Network System account.
Municipal Engineer to provide the GSO and City Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to assign one personnel who will diligently verify previous accounting entries and investigate the reasons for the accumulation of liability to GSIS over the years and effect the necessary adjustments if warranted.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to return immediately the unutilized funds to the NGAs concerned and the OIC – Municipal Accountant to prepare the reports to the different source agencies showing full settlement of the particular programs and projects and make the necessary accounting entries to correct the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Budget Officer to closely monitor/limit its expenses within the approved appropriations in order to avoid the incurrence of net loss/deficit;
Municipal Treasurer to report to the Local School Board the available cash from previous years’ SEF operations for consideration in the ensuing year’s budget preparation; and
Acting Municipal Accountant to adjust the long outstanding Due to NGAs account if found void with valid claimant so as to restore to SEF the cash allocated for the trust liability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1) OIC-Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers to adjust and record the correct balance of Trust Liability-LDRRMF account and provide adequate disclosure of the unutilized LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements at year-end; and
2) Municipal Treasurer to cause the transfer of cash to the Special Trust Fund- Trust Liability-LDRRM which should have a balance of ₱16,456,528.55 as of December 31, 2019.

We recommended that Management thru the GFPS:
capacitate the GAD Focal System thru attendance to webinars  conducted by DILG/PCW;
prepare the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; and
conduct a gender analysis of the identified PPAs through the administration of the tools prescribed by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01; and
maximize the implementation of the identified GAD projects to achieve the objectives of the government regarding the institutionalization of Gender and Development in the communities.

We recommended that the Management:
judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1; and
promptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as embodied in the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and/or staff concerned to:
1)   submit updated and complete BRS for all bank accounts of the Municipality, duly supported with the original copies of the BS, DMs/CMs, and cancelled/paid checks to facilitate review and verification thereof;
observations.reason: Bank Statements/Snapshots were not yet furnished to the Office of the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
4)  record the confirmed bank balances of the three bank accounts in the proper fund books; and
5)  set-up, maintain, and prepare individual BRS and subsidiary ledgers for these bank accounts to avoid understatement of the CIB-LCCA account at any given date.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to desist from granting additional cash advance if previous cash advance has not been fully liquidated.
observations.reason: Limited number of SDOs.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that Management cause the withholding of the payment of the salary if 30 days after due demand, no liquidation or explanation is received from the Accountable Officer, or the explanation received is not satisfactory and/or institute the filing of appropriate legal charges for failure to settle accounts.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursement basis for those AOs with unliquidated CA on travel.
observations.reason: Selective withholding of salary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to:
transfer/and or credit the amount of ₱7,673,563.86, representing the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from CYs 2017 and 2018, to a special trust liability account in the Trust Fund;
record such transfer by drawing up the appropriate accounting entries;

Error in some JEV to record adjustments.
Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct:

the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to submit the Audit Team paid DVs in CYs 2015 to 2018 totaling ₱12,277,260.66, together with their supporting documents; and
observations.reason: Some DVs were allegedly lost when Poblacion area was flooded due to typhoon.

observations.recommendation: both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to take hands-on and diligent review and supervision over delegated works to staff to improve and expedite submission of accounts and their supporting documents in the ensuing periods.

We recommended that Management strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Only projects funded from the continuing appropriations were mostly implemented in 2019.
observations.reason: Management was limited by the late approval of the appropriation ordinance and the conditions set therein.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.

We recommended that the Management:
coordinate and follow up with the Provincial Government of Camarines Sur as to the status of enforcement of the General Revision, in order to avoid the lax imposition of the supposedly increase in tax collections; and
direct the Municipal Assessor to make sure that the fair market values of the real properties, including the improvements, are properly reflected in the tax declarations and FAASs;
observations.managementAction: Management is still awaiting the response/approval by the SP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the concerned MIAC officers to refund the unauthorized honoraria received in CY 2017 and (b) stop giving honoraria to members of the MIAC in the ensuing years to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management will resume imposition of refund thru salary deduction.
observations.reason: Refund was discontinued since it was not closely monitored.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
require the refund of the irregular foreign travel expenses of ₱113,998.00 for lack of appropriation or specific statutory authority to incur such obligation in CY 2017. In the ensuing year/s, we also recommended that Management;
refrain from using public funds for foreign travel/s where no appropriations for the purpose exist; and
submit proper and necessary documentation for foreign travels, to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management: 
stop giving cash advances for payroll that exceed the amount of net pay for a given pay period;
refuse additional cash advances to DOs unless their excess cash advances have been fully refunded; and
require the attachment of the ‘computation for net pay’ or ‘list of payees showing their net pay’ to related cash advance vouchers to avoid suspensions and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management deny invitations to generic, repetitive, and redundant conventions, trainings, or seminars that entail large amounts of money for registration and travelling expenses.  Prior assessment or evaluation of participants’ qualifications, training needs, and available resources of the LGU should be made to ensure that only those conventions, trainings, or seminars that are highly relevant, mandatory, and responsive to the capacity-building needs or requirements of the LGU are attended to or participated in by the most qualified people in the LGU, to avoid any wasteful and inefficient utilization of scarce LGU funds.

We also recommended that Management demand immediate and full liquidation of unliquidated cash advances for conventions, trainings and/or seminars totaling ₱634,751.00 to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor revisit the LGU’s current MOA with CSPC and consider revocation or amendment of the terms stipulated therein. Let the CSPC maintain, operate, and shoulder the expenses of its own campus, in accordance with its mandate and in deference to the principle of local fiscal autonomy, to ease the current financial burden of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and other local officials concerned to submit the remaining documents needed for the lot purchase, particularly the SB Resolution authorizing the former Mayor to enter into a contract, proof of payment of seller’s taxes, and CAR, to avoid suspension in audit. We also recommended that Management investigate, review, and reassess the purchase price for possible excess or bias in the setting thereof, and thereafter adopt necessary measures to adjust the same, or even require full refund of any excess, if found unreasonable or excessive. Lastly, we recommended that subject lot acquisition be duly registered in the name of the Municipality to be able to perfect ownership and title thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Mayor and other concerned Offices have already taken initiatives to secure the required documents.
observations.reason: There is material impossibility on the part of the present Management to produce the rest of the documentary requirements to justify or clarify the audit findings. Neither the prior authority from the SB nor title of the purchased property can be produced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and/or staff concerned to (1) submit updated and complete BRS for all bank accounts of the Municipality, duly supported with the original copies of the BS, DMs/CMs, and cancelled/paid checks to facilitate review and verification thereof; (2) record all book reconciling items in the GL; and (3) communicate immediately to the bank all differences or discrepancies noted in the monthly BS for information and appropriate action.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) record the confirmed bank balances of the three bank accounts in the proper fund books and (b) set-up, maintain, and prepare individual BRS and subsidiary ledgers for these bank accounts to avoid understatement of the CIB-LCCA account at any given date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to correct the RPT and SET Receivables and their corresponding deferred accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to desist from granting additional cash advance if previous cash advance has not been fully liquidated.

We also recommended that Management cause the withholding of the payment of the salary if 30 days after due demand, no liquidation or explanation is received from the Accountable Officer, or the explanation received is not satisfactory and/or institute the filing of appropriate legal charges for failure to settle accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Service Office and Office of the Municipal Accountant to: (a) conduct analysis of the PPE accounts to identify, investigate and reconcile the accounts; (b) ensure all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the book of accounts; (c) prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items to reflect correct account balances, if warranted; (d) and maintain complete ledgers for each class of property, plant and equipment.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to: (1) transfer/and or credit the amount of ₱7,673,563.86, representing the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from CYs 2017 and 2018, to a special trust liability account in the Trust Fund;  (2) record such transfer by drawing up the appropriate accounting entries; and (3) provide adequate disclosure of the unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE in the Notes to Financial Statements at year-end.

We recommended that Management direct:
the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to submit the Audit Team paid DVs in CYs 2015 to 2018 totaling ₱12,277,260.66, together with their supporting documents; and
(b) both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to take hands-on and diligent review and supervision over delegated works to staff to improve and expedite submission of accounts and their supporting documents in the ensuing periods.

We recommended that Management strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: (1) trace/pinpoint possible errors in recording which may have caused the variance in reciprocal accounts; and (2) effect necessary correction/adjustment on said accounts to reflect their correct and reliable balances as of December 31, 2017 and onwards.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant is already in the process of reviewing LGU accounts and financial records to trace and correct or make adjustments of the same if necessary.
observations.reason: Review and evaluation of transactions and records to trace possible errors and records to trace possible errors requires considerble amount of time and manpower to fully determine the variance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the Accountant to recognize expenses out of cash advances in the period to which they relate; (b) direct all local officials and employees concerned to settle their unliquidated cash advances within fifteen days from receipt of demand; and (c) stop granting additional cash advances to employees unless the previous ones have been settled to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum and reminders have already been issued to all concerned Officials and personnel of the LGU Buhi for the purpose. No request for cash advance for travels and other similar nature have been granted especially to those with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of record of the Officials and employees granted with cash advances especially those covering the previous years’ cash advances is ongoing. A number of concerned Officials and employees have yet to comply with the liquidation directive. Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Treasury and Accounting Offices to submit the outstanding DVs and their supporting documents to the auditors within ten days from receipt of this letter to avoid suspensions in audit.  We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant take hands-on and diligent review and supervision of delegated works to staff to improve and expedite submission of accounts and their supporting documents in the ensuing periods, else they shall be held administratively liable for negligence and/or non-rendition of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Mayor already instructed both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to look into the matter. Partial submission of DVs were made in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Limited personnel in charge to collate and submit the subject unsubmitted DVs. Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) require all collecting officers to remit their collections intact and daily or within the period prescribed by existing regulations; (b) desist from issuing additional stubs of ORs to collectors unless the previous ones are first accounted for and remitted; (c) cause for the immediate revocation of designation as collecting officers against those who are performing incompatible functions and are habitually remiss in the remittance of their collections; and (d) recommend to the Municipal Mayor the institution of appropriate administrative disciplinary actions against the erring collectors.
observations.managementAction: Internal control has been initiated by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer particularly on the issuance of stubs of ORs. The Municipal Treasurer conducts monthly inventory of ORs to ensure the that all issued ORs are properly accounted and remitted by the respective Accountable Officers Reorganization of staffing structure have been carried out in order to ensure that the recommended measures are complied with. Erring employees were no longer given collection function but instead were assigned different tasks. Measures are currently underway to ensure that collections are being remitted daily/every other day, and deposited regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the concerned MIAC officers to refund the unauthorized honoraria received in CY 2017 and (b) stop giving honoraria to members of the MIAC in the ensuing years to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Directive were already issued to all concerned personnel. Partial refunds were already made.
observations.reason: Other concerned personnel have yet to make refund of the unauthorized honoraria received. Management agreed to make the necessary arrangement with the concerned personnel for the withholding of said amount from their salary and other monetary benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) require the refund of the irregular foreign travel expenses of ₱113,998.00 for lack of appropriation or specific statutory authority to incur such obligation in CY 2017. In the ensuing year/s, we also recommended that Management (b) refrain from using public funds for foreign travel/s where no appropriations for the purpose exist and (c) submit proper and necessary documentation for foreign travels, to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management has refrained from availing foreign travel/s without appropriations.
observations.reason: One-time payment/refund of the foreign travel expense is taxing on the part of the personnel concerned of all concerned. They asked that refund thereof be made on staggered basis. They agreed to deduct the said amount from their monthly salary and other monetary benefits until July 2019.

observations.recommendation: To avoid the penalties for non-compliance with certain provisions of RA No. 9003, we recommended that Management: (a) convert its controlled dump into a sanitary landfill with minimum provisions for liners, leachate treatment, gas control, ground water monitoring system, and covers; (b) allocate sufficient funds for the establishment of an MRF before the end of CY 2018 or 2019; (c) enforce the mandatory segregation policy in garbage collection and transport; and (d) provide protective gears to all garbage collectors.
observations.managementAction: Safe closure procedure of the dump site was initiated. MRF have been established at Municipal and barangay level. Garbage collectors have been provided with safety gears.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) refrain from using public funds for media advertisements or sponsorships that only cater to publicity or private propaganda and are outside of the LGU’s statutory mandate or mission; and (b) submit relevant documentation for the advertising expenses incurred in CY 2017 to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Advertisements have already been discouraged if not totally disallowed as an internal control to disbursement of funds. Radio segment sponsorship was however allowed as part of the IEC strategy of LGU Buhi i.e. tax campaign, public announcements etc. and for other public service announcement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (1) stop giving cash advances for payroll that exceed the amount of net pay for a given pay period; (2) refuse additional cash advances to DOs unless their excess cash advances have been fully refunded; and (3) require the attachment of the ‘computation for net pay’ or ‘list of payees showing their net pay’ to related cash advance vouchers to avoid suspensions and/or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management adopted the audit recommendations of the Team. Cash advances granted to Accountable Officers are equivalent to net pay for a given pay period.
observations.reason: Special Disbursing Officer have yet to liquidate her Cash Advances which remained outstanding for over one year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) make a complete and honest inventory and reporting of infrastructure accomplishments in its QRP and (b) install proper tarpaulin signboards at project sites, to notify the public of the essential features of GPPAs that are being implemented by the LGU in a given year/s.
observations.managementAction: Prescribed notices in compliance of the applicable CoA Circular (design, size and content) have been posted in the projects being undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize and fast-track the implementation and completion, preferably within the budget year, of all, not just a few, priority development projects/programs funded out of the 20% EDF appropriations in a given year to attain the desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes of the Fund and thereby uplift the living conditions of the general public.
observations.managementAction: Management re-strategized in the implementation of the 20% EDF funded projects considering that the FY 2017 Budget was authorized only on the 2nd quarter of the year.
observations.reason: There was considerable delay in the approval of the Municipal Budget, hence, the delay in the implementation of the identified projects. Remaining unimplemented projects were not fully undertaken in CY 2018 due to some restrictions by the SB. Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BuB focal persons to fast track the implementation of PPAs covered by the CY 2017 BuB Funds in order to (a) accomplish the specific purposes for which the Funds were granted and (b) deliver immediate and optimum results to target beneficiaries of the BuB Program. Delay in the implementation of the planned PPAs must be investigated, resolved, even admonished, in order to avoid unnecessary returns or reversions of much-needed Funds to grantor-partner agencies.  We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit necessary liquidation reports and supporting documents for the partially utilized BuB Funds totalling ₱22.614 million.
observations.managementAction: Management also re-strategized in the implementation of the BuB Projects especially those with corresponding fund counterpart charged to FY 2017 Budget in order to compensate the lost time due to the delay in the passsage of the CY2017 Budget. Procurement plans and ongoing implementation schedules were reviewed and accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and/or staff concerned to (1) submit updated and complete BRS for all bank accounts of the Municipality, duly supported with the original copies of the BS, DMs/CMs, and cancelled/paid checks to facilitate review and verification thereof; (2) record all book reconciling items in the GL; and (3) communicate immediately to the bank all differences or discrepancies noted in the monthly BS for information and appropriate action. We also recommended that Management consider closing all dormant bank accounts which are no longer needed or useful in operations, to avoid unnecessary bank charges and to lessen the burden of the Accounting Office in keeping records of, and preparing reconciliation statements for, technically useless accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had submitted current BRS for all bank accounts in the GF, TF, and SEF, except for the main bank account in the GF-Proper.
observations.reason: Prior years’ records are not available to facilitate preparation of BRS for the GF-Proper bank account in the current year. Also, BRS submitted for some bank accounts did not include necessary attachments, like original copies of Bank Statements, paid/cancelled checks returned by bank, and DM/CMs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management deny invitations to generic, repetitive, and redundant conventions, trainings, or seminars that entail large amounts of money for registration and travelling expenses. Prior assessment or evaluation of participants’ qualifications, training needs, and available resources of the LGU should be made to ensure that only those conventions, trainings, or seminars that are highly relevant, mandatory, and responsive to the capacity-building needs or requirements of the LGU are attended to by the most qualified people in the LGU, to avoid any wasteful and inefficient utilization of scarce LGU funds.  We also recommended that Management demand immediate and full liquidation of unliquidated cash advances for conventions, trainings and/or seminars totaling P634,751.00 to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Attendance to conferences, trainings and conventions with repetitive topics were already discouraged. Personnel mechanism whereby a Training and CapDev Committee was organized to assist in determining which trainings are necessary.
observations.reason: Liquidation/refund of the related cash advances were not yet fully enforced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor revisit the LGU’s current MOA with CSPC and consider revocation or amendment of the terms stipulated therein. Let the CSPC maintain, operate, and shoulder the expenses of its own campus, in accordance with its mandate and in deference to the principle of local fiscal autonomy, to ease the current financial burden of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Honoraria, salaries or wages were no longer being paid by the Management. Only the rental for the building being utilized and utilities are being assumed by the LGU in compliance with the agreement embodied in the MOA between the parties.
observations.reason: Management already proposed a revisit of the MoA. While the same is valid and parties thereto bind to the terms and conditions therein, the LGU is in agreement with the CSPC for its deliverables before a new MoA can be discussed.

beneficiaries of the BuB Program. Delay in the implementation of the
planned PPAs must be investigated, resolved, even admonished, in order
to avoid unnecessary returns or reversions of much-needed Funds to
grantor-partner agencies. We also recommended that Management direct
the Municipal Accountant to submit necessary liquidation reports and
supporting documents for the partially utilized BuB Funds totalling
$22.614 million.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and/or staff concerned to (1) submit  updated and complete BRS for all bank accounts of the Municipality, duly supported with the original copies of the BS, DMs/CMs, and cancelled/paid  checks ~to facilitate  review  and verification  thereof, (2) record all book reconciling items in the GL; and (3) communicate immediately to the bank all differences or discrepancies noted in the monthly BS for information and appropriate action.

We also recommended that Management consider closing all dormant bank
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had submitted current BRS for all bank accounts in the GF, TF, and SEF, except for the main bank account in the GF-Proper.
observations.reason:
Prior years’ records are not available to facilitate preparation of BRS for the GF-Proper bank account in the current year.

Also, BRS submitted for some bank accounts difl not include necessary attachments, like original copies of Bank Statements, paid/cancelled  checks returned by bank, and DM/CMs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) record the confirmed bank balances of the three bank accounts in the Trust Fund and (b) set-up, maintain, and prepare individual BRS and subsidiary ledgers for these bank accounts to avoid understatement of the CIB-LCCA account at any given date.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjustments were already made by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to submit the CY 2016 paid DVs and related supporting documents to the Audit Team to facilitate audit and examination thereof. Non-submission of paid vouchers shall be a cause for suspension or disallowance of related claims in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had only made partial and incomplete submission of accounts in CY 2016 and 2017, which  resulted in unsubmitted DVs totaling P4.875 million as of latest tally by the auditors in  March, 2018.
observations.reason: Delayed and incomplete submission of supporting documents by payees and other officials involved in the processing and payment of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, respectively, to: (1) transfer the amount of P6,016,218.27, representing the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from CYs 2012 to 2016, to a special trust liability account in the Trust Fund; and (2) maintain separate subsidiary ledgers per source year to keep track of the balances of these special trust funds within the 5-year validity period fixed by law.
observations.managementAction: Complete fund transfer of unexpended CF from prior years was already effected in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and other local officials were concerned to collate and to submit to the auditors all lacking documents - for the assailed land acquisition, to avoid suspension/s in audit.
observations.managementAction: Several (but not all) documents related to the land acquisition were already submitted to the auditors in CY 2017.
observations.reason: Incumbent officials| cannot produce the SB| Resolution  authorizing| former Mayor Lacoste to| enter into a Contract of| Lot Purchase in CY 2016.  Likewise, the CGT and the DST that were previously deducted from the full payment to seller in March, 2016 were not yet remitted to the BIR until today.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor limit the cash advance for CF to the 3-month requirement allowed by regulations.and ensure that (a) subsequent disbursement and reporting of cash advances will be done- on-a quarterly basis and (b) no cash advance for CF will be wused as reimbursement for confidential expenses incurred prior to the grant thereof. We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant _to submit the Annual and/or Supplemental Budgets showing the specific and itemized appropriation for POP as basis for the 30% allocation for CF, to avoid any suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The incumbent Mayor did.not draw any cash advance for| Confidential Expenses in CY 2017. Instead, specific appropriations and disbursements for| the  purpose  were directly made to peace and order programs and activities identified during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require her collectors to remit their collections daily in accordance with Section 29 of NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Collectors were advised by the Municipal Treasurer to make daily and full remittance  of  their collections, but only a few obeyed. It Reiterated by the Municipal Treasurer that she can hardly discipline collectors who already developed a bad habit of not remitting collections intact and daily to the MT.
observations.reason: Erring  collectors  have never been sanctioned by higher authorities, thus they continue to be remiss in their duties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management deny CY invitations to generic, repetitive, and redundant conventions, trainings, or seminars that entail large amounts of money for registration and travelling expenses. Prior assessment or evaluation of participants’ qualifications, training needs, and available resources of the LGU should be made to ensure that only those conventions, trainings, or seminars that are highly relevant; mandatory, and responsive to the capacity-building needs or requirements of the LGU are attended to or participated in by the most qualified people in the LGU, to avoid any wasteful and inefficient utilization of scarce LGU funds. We also recommended that Management demand immediate and full liquidation of unliquidated cash advances for conventions, trainings and/or seminars totaling P634,751.00 to  avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Annual league conferences were still attended to by elective officials without regard to economy and necessity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor revisit the LGU’s current MOA with CSPC and consider revocation or amendment of the terms stipulated therein. Let the CSPC maintain, operate, and shoulder the expenses of its own campus, in  accordance with its mandate-and-in-deference-to-the principle of local fiscal autonomy, to ease the current financial burden of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management had still allowed the charging off  some maintenance and operating expenses of| the CSPC Extension| Campus against the funds of the LGU in| CY 2017,
observations.reason: Compliance with the terms of the MOA, which Management has not yet revisited and/or revoked despite recommendations by the auditors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct all payees to —refund the unauthorized BAC honoraria of P35,000.00 each, without prejudice to the personal liability of the official/s who approved the unsubstantiated and illegal expenditure in the General Fund totaling to P455,000.00.
observations.managementAction: BAC Members have refunded the unauthorized honoraria totaling P455,000.00 in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation and completion, preferably within the budget year, of all priority development projects/programs funded out of the 20% EDF to attain the desired outcomes of the Fund and thereby uplift the living conditions of the general public.
observations.managementAction: 12 out of 18 development projects were already completed. (Note: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR)
observations.reason: Delayed and sluggish development of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BuB focal persons to fast track the implementation of PPAs covered by the CY 2015 DOT-BuB Funds, in order that (a) the purposes for which the Funds were granted be accomplished and (b) immediate and optimum results be delivered to target beneficiaries of the BuB Program. Delay in the implementation of the planned PPAs must be investigated, resolved, even admonished, in order to avoid unnecessary returns or reversions of much-needed Funds to grantor-partner agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management had: 1. Instructed the focal person to expedite the staff implementation. 2. Augmented the personnel finding through the designation of Tourism Officer in order to fast track all pertinent undertakings relating to the BUB projects. 3. Sought legal assistance to facilitate the processing of the deed of donation concerning the site of the tourist facilities.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of reports or outputs by the BuB focal person and slow processing of related documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit all lacking documents for the BAC honoraria paid in CY 2015, such as: Minutes of BAC meetings, Attendance Sheets, NOASs relating to the procurement activities being claimed, and Certification that the procurement involved competitive bidding, to establish regularity of the claims and payment thereof. Non-submission of these documents shall be a cause for the suspension of said claims in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Chairman of the BAC was already advised to submit the documents the soonest possible time.
observations.reason: Lack of commitment to comply with certain auditing rules and regulations, particularly on the requirement for proper and complete documentation of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review, analyze and evaluate the Bank Reconciliation Statements thoroughly. Likewise, the reconciling items requiring adjustment be booked up to correct the Cash in Bank Local Currency account balance. We also recommended that Bank Reconciliation Statement be prepared for Bank Account No. 2772-1007-63.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had exerted efforts to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts, except for the preparation of the BRS for the LBP Account Nos. 2772-1007-63, 2772-1077-68, 2772-1068-00, and 2772-1068-42. There was difficulty tracing/reconciling the transactions on the GF-Proper bank account, considering the length of time (since 2005) that preparation of the BRS for this account had been avoided or put off.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report. There was difficulty tracing/reconciling the transactions on the GF-Proper bank account, considering the length of time (since 2005) that preparation of the BRS for this account had been avoided or put off.

observations.managementAction: Updated BRS were already submitted in CY 2016, except for necessary attachments like original copies of Bank Statements, paid/cancelled checks returned by bank, and DM/CMs. Dormant bank accounts still exist up to this writing.
