observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report. BRS submitted for some bank accounts did not include necessary attachments like original copies of Bank Statements, paid/cancelled checks returned by bank, and DM/CMs. Dormant bank accounts still exist up to this writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, respectively, to: (1) transfer the amount of P10.937 million, representing the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from CYs 2011 to 2015, to a special trust liability account in the Trust Fund; (2) provide adequate disclosure of the unutilized QRF and DRRMF-MOOE at year-end; and (3) maintain separate subsidiary ledgers per source and year to keep track of the balances of these special trust funds within the 5-year validity period fixed by law.
observations.managementAction: Management officials, who were present during the exit conference, have informed the auditors that they have already deposited the amount of P10,518,380.30 to cover the actual transfer of unexpended CY 2011 to 2016 LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund. However, no written proof of such transfer was yet submitted to the auditors, for reference and guidance in audit.
observations.reason: Partially Reiterated in Part I of this Report. Excess amount transferred to the TF, representing the unutilized CY 2011 LDRRMF, should be reverted back to the GF and made available for regular appropriations in the subsequent year/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and all others concerned to submit the CY 2015 paid DVs and related supporting documents to the Audit Team to facilitate audit and examination thereof and to determine the validity and regularity of related transactions.
observations.managementAction: Significant amount of paid DVs in CY 2015 were already submitted in CY 2016. But, a similar infraction was noted in CY 2016, where the amount of unsubmitted DVs reached a high of P14.045 million.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report. Delayed and incomplete submission of supporting documents by payees and other officials involved in the processing and payment of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (1) direct all local officials and employees concerned to settle their unliquidated cash advances to avoid any suspension or disallowance in audit; and (2) stop granting additional cash advances to employees unless the previous ones have been settled. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the liquidation of prior years’ cash advances for CF totaling P4.5 million, pursuant to Item 6.2.2.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
observations.managementAction: Partial liquidations of outstanding cash advances were made by some accountable officers and employees in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Due from Officers & Employees was reduced by 67%.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the local Sanggunian to (1) justify the arbitrary appropriations for CF in CYs 2011 to 2015 despite the cited deficiencies; and (2) take caution or, better yet, refrain from making future appropriations for CF, unless the conditions and documentation required by JC No. 2015-01, supra, are first complied with, to avoid any suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Justification relative to the Certification from the PNP Chief was already submitted to COA Central Office, copy furnished the Office of the Resident Auditor. But, a similar infraction was noted in the appropriation and use of CY 2016 CF amounting to P1.5M.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modifications in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately, the CAV and related supporting documents for the P1 million CF cash advance of the Municipal Mayor in CY 2015, to avoid any suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The CAV, together with the dummy check and ObR, was already submitted to Office of the Audit Team in prior years sometime in May, 2016. But, there were still other documents that remained unsubmitted until today, such as: (1) Annual or Supplemental Budget in CY 2015 showing the specific and itemized appropriation for POP; (2) Certified copy of the minutes of the meeting evidencing the 2/3 votes of the LPOC approving the POP and the release of the CF; and others.
observations.reason: Non-existent appropriation for POP in the Annual or Supplemental Budgets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require immediate refund and remittance to the BIR of excessive BAC Honoraria totaling P0.059 million. In the ensuing years, we also recommended that Management maintain the taxability of all forms of 'honoraria' and deduct correct and appropriate withholding taxes from the claims for honoraria of the BAC, BAC Secretariat, and TWG members using the applicable tax rates provided under RR No. 10-2008, supra. The withholding must be outright, meaning, immediately and directly deducted from the claims, to avoid any slipup or oversight in taxation which may lead to many unwanted consequences (i.e. interest, surcharge, and penalties) in the future. Taxes not withheld constitute excess payments to claimants, thus, disallowable in audit.
observations.managementAction: BAC Members have refunded the amount of taxes corresponding to their honoraria. However, Management did not remit the taxes withheld pending reconciliation of Due to BIR account with actual remittances.
observations.reason: The Chairman of the BAC was already advised to submit the documents the soonest possible time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management limit the cash advances of the DO to the maximum cash accountability covered by her fidelity bond. Any increase in accountability, which requires corresponding increase in the 'amount of bond' and 'bond premium', must be approved by the agency head and conveyed to the Bureau of Treasury for appropriate incremental bonding.
observations.managementAction: To avoid further excessive cash advances (in relation to the DO’s maximum accountability), Management had approved, and is now in the process of, Payroll Automation. Salaries of regular employees were paid thru ATM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation and completion, preferably within the budget year, of all priority development projects/programs funded out of the 20% EDF to attain the desired outcomes of the Fund and thereby uplift the living conditions of the general public.
observations.reason: Some projects were not implemented due to unavailability of land where the buildings may be constructed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BuB focal persons to fast track the implementation of PPAs covered by the CY 2015 DOT-BuB Funds, in order to (a) accomplish the purposes for which the Funds were granted and (b) deliver immediate and optimum results to target beneficiaries of the BuB Program. Delay in the implementation of the planned PPAs must be investigated, resolved, even admonished, in order to avoid unnecessary returns or reversions of much-needed funds to grantor-partner agencies.
observations.managementAction: 1. Instructed the focal person to expedite the implementation. 2. Augmented the personnel complement through the designation of Tourism Officer in order to fast track all pertinent undertakings relating to the BuB projects. 3. Sought legal assistance to facilitate the processing of the deed of donation concerning the site of the tourist facilities.
observations.reason: Delayed and sluggish implementation of projects. 12 out of 18 development projects were already completed. Partially delayed submission of reports or outputs by the focal person and staff.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that cash advances be granted, utilized and liquidated in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002. Require the LGU Officials & Employees concerned to liquidate/settle them immediately and refrain from granting additional cash advances for accounts. We also recommended that appropriate sanction be instituted for failure of the officials and employees to observe the existing regulations on the liquidation of cash advance after they are properly notified through issuance of demand letters. We further recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the subsidiary ledgers of all accountable officers with the general ledger of Due from Officers and Employees account, all funds. Prepare and effect the necessary correcting/adjusting entries to reconcile these two reciprocal ledgers to finally determine the correct ending balance.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant had made repeated verbal demands from officers and employees concerned, especially the elected officials, but to no avail. The Municipal Mayor has instructed the Accountant to send written demands, as preliminary to any future legal action.
observations.reason: Verbal demands were often ignored by persons concerned. Hence, the Municipal Mayor has instructed the Accountant to send written demands, as preliminary to any future legal action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a periodic inventory of all the properties of the LGU noting down the location, persons accountable and the physical condition of the property. The committee should prepare and certify correct the RPCPPE based on actual inventory taking of each office and submit the same to COA. Thereafter, the RPCPPE must be reconciled with the property records of each class of property at the GSO and subsidiary ledgers maintained at the accounting office. The Accountant should maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards to support GL account balances and the OIC-General Services Officer to keep corresponding property cards for control purposes. These records should contain adequate details/description of the properties such as model, serial number, cost and other pertinent information to facilitate identification, its ARE must be renewed regularly pursuant to existing regulations. Retired/resigned employees should be required to secure clearance from property accountability and the corresponding ARE for returned items should be cancelled accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to comply with this recommendation. Accounting personnel who were once assigned to the task have been re-assigned to other offices, thus, preventing the completion/reconciliation of the inventory report. As to the unreconciled balance of PPE per books and per inventory reports, Management can no longer locate documents pertaining to prior years’ acquisitions of equipment.
observations.reason: Not Implemented. Accounting personnel who were once assigned to the task have been re-assigned to other offices, thus, preventing the completion/reconciliation of the inventory report. As to the unreconciled balance of PPE per books and per inventory reports, Management can no longer locate documents pertaining to prior years’ acquisitions of equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management; 1. Reconstitute the Solid Waste Management Board to conform to the membership requirements of the Act and to optimize the involvement of civil society and other sectors as envisioned in the law. 2. Cause the proper labeling/markings of designated containers or receptacles in various collection points for proper identification as to each type of waste. More so, the use of separate schedules and/or separate garbage trucks or haulers shall be required for specific types of waste or vehicles to be used to transport solid waste should have appropriate compartments to facilitate efficient collection and transport of solid waste. 3. Adopt appropriate control measures to ensure that the sanitary landfill conform with the requirements prescribe by Article g of the Act and to cause the establishment of the material recovery facility for the efficient management of solid wastes of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management had already acquired a 3.6~ hectare lot to be used as future site of its proposed Solid Waste Eco Park in Barangay Salvacion, Buhi, Camarines Sur.
observations.reason: Funds were not yet allocated for the actual development of the land and construction of the proposed Solid Waste Eco Park thereat.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit job order contracts and other pertinent documents as an integral part of the payrolls. We also recommended that management assess its existing organizational structure so that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established, thereby avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job order employees. Further, we recommended that the hiring of job order personnel be resorted to base on the necessity and urgency of the agency’s need.
observations.managementAction: Instructions given to the Human Resource Officer to comply with the documentary requirements for JO Payroll.
observations.reason: Not Implemented. Some JOs did not submit their DTRs and Accomplishment Reports on time. Hiring of job order personnel was not diminished for reasons only known to the appointing authority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to limit appropriations and expenditures for Personal Services within the 45% limitations prescribed under Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160. Compute the budget allocation for Personal Services using actual Regular Income of the next preceding year.
observations.managementAction: There was a significant reduction in the CY 2016 PS excess, as shown in the Sangguniang Panlalawigan’s review of the Municipality’s CY 2015 & 2016 Annual Budgets. Letters of Levy from the PTO were also sent, combined with the auction sales of some delinquent properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to strictly submit all paid disbursements from LDRRMF and submit necessary/ required supporting documents, otherwise the team shall be constrained to suspend/disallow in post-audit the said transactions.
We recommended that management design effective tax strategies to intensify collections from taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: The MTO had sent various Notices of Delinquencies to taxpayers. Demand letters were sent.
observations.reason: The Municipality had registered a low collection efficiency in the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and all others who have a direct hand in the processing, approval, and payment of transactions to strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the submission of paid disbursement vouchers including their supporting documents to the Auditor as required under COA Circular No. 95-006 and Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant had endeavored to submit the lacking CY 2014 vouchers in the succeeding years.
observations.reason: There were still few unsubmitted DVs from prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the projects embodied in the 20% Development Fund be implemented immediately for the land is available and considering that there are funds allocated for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: Management had started to secure pertinent documents for the land where the buildings may be constructed.
observations.reason: Some projects were not implemented due to unavailability of land where the buildings may be constructed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to settle immediately all audit suspensions and to submit without delay to the Office of the Auditor the unsubmitted vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Partial settlements were made in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Partial settlements were made in CY 2016. Some documents that were subject of audit were made in CY 2016. Partial settlements were made; while persons responsible and/or liable simply ignored or brushed off the Notices.
